---
title: "Dongwook Kim"
position: "Ph.D. Student"
sortby: "Kim, Dongwook"
started: "Fall 2024"
ended: "Current"
toc: false
about:
    id: person-profile
    image: "../../_assets/img/people/dongwook.jpeg"
    template: trestles
    links:
        -   icon: envelope
            text: Email
            href: mailto:dongwook.kim@rice.edu
        -   icon: github
            text: Github
            href: https://github.com/hydkim
        -   icon: linkedin
            text: LinkedIN
            href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dongwookkimwater/
        -   icon: graduation-cap
            text: Google Scholar
            href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=VR9cB8EAAAAJ&view_op=list_works&sortby=pubdate
        -   icon: globe
            text: Personal Website
            href: https://hydkim.github.io/

format: 
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

:::{#person-profile}
## Bio

Dongwook Kim is a Ph.D. student in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering at Rice University.
His research integrates knowledge and methodologies from various disciplines, including hydrology, climate science, statistics, and machine learning, to quantify hydroclimate risk due to climate change and to develop effective mitigation strategies.
His current research focuses on flood extent mapping in urban and rural environments using synthetic aperture radar (SAR) and deep learning.
Dongwook is coadvised by [Prof. Avantika Gori](https://gori.blogs.rice.edu/).

## Education
- **Hanyang University** | Seoul, Republic of Korea<br />MS in Civil and Environmental System Engineering, 2021
- **Hanyang University ERICA** | Ansan, Republic of Korea<br />BS in Civil Engineering, 2019
:::
